reasoning:
The stock (T) just reported positive Q3 earnings on October 22, 2025, beating subscriber expectations. This gives a positive momentum tailwind over the short-term. However, forecasting stock prices over short horizons largely follows a random walk with a small positive drift (the equity risk premium). 

For the November dates, no ex-dividend drops are expected, as the Q3 ex-dividend date passed on October 10. Given the slightly positive market drift and recent earnings beat, the short-term probability of trading higher is slightly above 50% (0.54 for the 1-week horizon, 0.52 as uncertainty expands for 1-month). 

For the January 24, 2026 resolution date, AT&T typically has a Q4 ex-dividend date in early January (comparable to January 2025 for a February payout). This ~$0.28 per share markdown (~1% of the stock price) will mechanically lower the share price shortly before the late January resolution. This largely counteracts the positive expected return over a 3-month horizon, bringing the probability closer to 50% (0.51) and satisfying the requirement that longer-horizon forecasts revert closer to maximum uncertainty.
